topic: uv — the Astral Python package & project manager

questions.q: What programming language is uv written in, and what does Astral describe uv as?
questions.gold: uv is written in Rust and is described as 'an extremely fast Python package and project manager.'

questions.q: What is the exact command shown to install uv on Windows using the standalone installer?
In uv's tools guide, the uvx command is documented as being exactly equivalent to which uv subcommand?
questions.gold: uvx is exactly equivalent to 'uv tool run' (uvx is an alias for uv tool run).

questions.q: According to the docs, which existing Python tools does uv aim to replace, and what speed improvement over pip is claimed?
questions.gold: uv replaces pip, pip-tools, pipx, poetry, pyenv, twine, and virtualenv, and claims to be 10-100x faster than pip.

questions.q: What specific uv version is used in the installation page's example for installing a pinned/specific version via the standalone installer?
questions.gold: Version 0.11.22, e.g. curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/0.11.22/install.sh | sh (and the equivalent Windows PowerShell command with /0.11.22/install.ps1).

questions.q: Which built-in cloud deployment command does uv provide to push a project directly to AWS Lambda or Heroku?
questions.gold: Unsupported — the sources do not mention any cloud deployment command or AWS Lambda/Heroku integration in uv. uv is a package, project, tool, and Python-version manager; no such deploy feature is described.
